canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, AGGREFACT is consistently described as a benchmark dataset used to evaluate factual consistency within the task of summarization. The prevailing operationalization of the benchmark involves the CNN/DM and XSum datasets, a detail mentioned in multiple sources. One common definition further specifies that AGGREFACT provides "fine-grained annotations and summary-level labels across multiple domains" to facilitate its evaluation of factual consistency. In practice, it is used alongside other instruments like TRUE and SUMMAC, as noted in one paper's introduction: "tested on rich benchmark datasets, such as TRUE... SUMMAC... and AGGREFACT" (COVE:COntext andVEracity prediction for out-of-context images). Some work also refers to using specific data splits, such as "the AGGREFACT-FTSOTA split".
papers: AgentSense: Benchmarking Social Intelligence of Language Agents through Interactive Scenarios
Automatically Discovering How Misogyny is Framed on Social Media
COVE:COntext andVEracity prediction for out-of-context images

definitions.text: Benchmark dataset for evaluating factual consistency in summarization, providing fine-grained annotations and summary-level labels across multiple domains.
definitions.paper: AgentSense: Benchmarking Social Intelligence of Language Agents through Interactive Scenarios

definitions.text: A benchmark for summary evaluation that includes the CNN and XSum datasets, focusing on factual consistency.
definitions.paper: Automatically Discovering How Misogyny is Framed on Social Media
